---

## 1) Purpose

- **Problem:** RelyLoop's "engine-neutral" positioning is rhetorical until a third OSS engine ships. Today the loop runs on Elasticsearch and OpenSearch (`ElasticAdapter` per [`elastic.py`](../../../../../backend/app/adapters/elastic.py)). Apache Solr is the third canonical OSS search engine (OSC + Sease + Querqy + Haystack reference stack); without it, "all three OSS engines" is a marketing claim no operator can verify against the codebase. The just-shipped UBI path (`feat_ubi_judgments`, PR #317, 2026-05-29) extends to a third engine the moment a Protocol-conformant Solr adapter lands — Solr ships `solr.UBIComponent` in core writing the same `ubi_queries`/`ubi_events` schema the live `UbiReader` already consumes, so zero new UBI code is in scope here.
- **Outcome:** A single `SolrAdapter` implements the `SearchAdapter` Protocol against Apache Solr 9.x and 10.x (both SolrCloud and standalone), pivoting on a capability probe at construction time. Relevance engineers can register a Solr cluster, see its schema and targets (cores or collections), run ad-hoc `edismax` queries, generate judgments via either LLM or UBI converters, and ship a winning configuration as a Solr-engine PR — the full Karpathy loop, identical to ES/OpenSearch. The "engine-neutral" claim in [`docs/07_research/comparison.md`](../../../../../docs/07_research/comparison.md) and the umbrella spec §1 becomes factual.
- **Non-goal:** No LTR training (consume-only is in scope; training stays in the backlog per [`adapters.md` §"Reserved for later releases"](../../../../01_architecture/adapters.md)). No Solr 8.x or earlier (out of scope; both 9 and 10 maintain `edismax` + Schema API parity). No native Solr non-OSS extensions (Lucidworks Fusion features beyond the OSS Solr core). No webhook / observability work — those layer on at MVP3.

## 2) Current state audit

### Existing implementations

- [`backend/app/adapters/protocol.py`](../../../../../backend/app/adapters/protocol.py) — `SearchAdapter` Protocol + Pydantic types. `EngineType = Literal["elasticsearch", "opensearch"]` at line 31 is the wire-value source of truth; **this Literal must extend to include `"solr"`** as part of this feature (Story A6). The docstring at lines 9–13 already names `SolrAdapter` as the MVP2 adapter — code follows.
- [`backend/app/adapters/elastic.py`](../../../../../backend/app/adapters/elastic.py) — the only existing adapter (880 lines). `ElasticAdapter` (line 96) is the pattern reference; its constants at lines 63–82 (`SUPPORTED_ENGINE_TYPES`, `SUPPORTED_AUTH_KINDS`, `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE`) are the source-of-truth allowlists this feature extends. Public methods this spec must mirror on the Solr side: `__init__` (101), `_request` (139), `health_check` (~244), `list_targets` (366), `get_schema` (434), `list_query_parsers` (496), `render` (531), `search_batch` (566), `explain` (671), `get_document` (712), `list_documents` (777).
- [`backend/app/adapters/errors.py`](../../../../../backend/app/adapters/errors.py) — `ClusterUnreachableError`, `TargetNotFoundError`, `TargetsForbiddenError`, `InvalidQueryDSLError`, `QueryTimeoutError`. SolrAdapter raises the same classes for parity (router translation is engine-agnostic).
- [`backend/app/adapters/credentials.py`](../../../../../backend/app/adapters/credentials.py) — `resolve_credentials(auth_kind, credentials_ref)`. Extends with `solr_basic` (YAML `{username, password}`) and `solr_apikey` (YAML `{jwt_token, refresh_url?}`); the credential file shapes are already documented in [`adapters.md` §"Authentication and credentials"](../../../../01_architecture/adapters.md).
- [`backend/app/db/models/cluster.py`](../../../../../backend/app/db/models/cluster.py) — `Cluster` ORM model. CHECK constraints at lines 33–44 enforce `engine_type IN ('elasticsearch', 'opensearch')` and `auth_kind IN ('es_apikey', 'es_basic', 'opensearch_basic', 'opensearch_sigv4')`. Both extend in this feature's migration.
- [`backend/app/api/v1/clusters.py`](../../../../../backend/app/api/v1/clusters.py) — `POST /api/v1/clusters`, `GET /api/v1/clusters[/{id}]`, `DELETE`, `GET .../schema`, `POST .../run_query`. Routes are engine-agnostic; they will accept `engine_type=solr` without code change once the Pydantic enum and the adapter dispatch pick it up.
- [`backend/app/services/cluster.py`](../../../../../backend/app/services/cluster.py) — adapter dispatch lives here. Today `build_adapter(cluster)` at line 325 unconditionally instantiates `ElasticAdapter` for both `elasticsearch` and `opensearch`; needs a branch on `engine_type == "solr"` that returns `SolrAdapter` instead. The async `acquire_adapter(cluster)` context manager at line 237 wraps `build_adapter` and is consumed by every service that needs an adapter — both stay engine-agnostic at the call site once `build_adapter` learns the third branch.
- [`ui/src/lib/enums.ts:40`](../../../../../ui/src/lib/enums.ts#L40) — `ENGINE_TYPE_VALUES = ['elasticsearch', 'opensearch'] as const`. Extends to include `'solr'` per the Enumerated Value Contract Discipline (CLAUDE.md).
- [`ui/src/lib/enums.ts:44`](../../../../../ui/src/lib/enums.ts#L44) — `AUTH_KIND_VALUES`. Extends with `solr_basic`, `solr_apikey`.
- [`ui/src/components/clusters/register-cluster-modal.tsx`](../../../../../ui/src/components/clusters/register-cluster-modal.tsx) — cluster-registration form. Engine + auth-kind dropdowns already grounded in the backend allowlist; extension is mechanical once the allowlists grow.
- [`ui/src/components/clusters/ubi-onramp-nudge.tsx`](../../../../../ui/src/components/clusters/ubi-onramp-nudge.tsx) — shipped by `feat_ubi_judgments` Capability B. Currently engine-aware for ES + OpenSearch; extend the per-engine copy to cover Solr's `solr.UBIComponent` enablement steps.
- [`docker-compose.yml`](../../../../../docker-compose.yml) — existing `elasticsearch` + `opensearch` services bound to `127.0.0.1:9200` / `:9201`. New `solr` service added at `127.0.0.1:8983` mirroring the same shape (named volume, healthcheck, secrets via `*_FILE` mounts).
- [`samples/products.json`](../../../../../samples/products.json) — the 432 KB Amazon ESCI sample dataset MVP1 uses for ES. The Solr Compose service seeds a `products` collection from the same file.
- [`samples/ubi_index_mappings.json`](../../../../../samples/ubi_index_mappings.json) — canonical UBI index mapping shipped by `feat_demo_ubi_study_comparison` (PR #320). The Solr integration tests reuse it for UBI collection bootstrap.
- [`docs/08_guides/tutorial-first-study.md`](../../../../../docs/08_guides/tutorial-first-study.md) — currently has Step 0 Path A (cloud OpenAI) and Path B (local LLM). This feature adds **Path C** ("run the tutorial against Solr instead of Elasticsearch").
- [`docs/03_runbooks/`](../../../../../docs/03_runbooks/) — `cluster-registration.md`, `ubi-judgment-generation.md` already exist. This feature adds `solr-cluster-registration.md`.

### Existing behaviors affected by scope change

- **Cluster registration form `engine_type` dropdown.** Current: 2 options. New: 3 options (Solr appears). Decision needed: **no** — additive.
- **Cluster registration form `auth_kind` dropdown.** Current: 4 options (`es_apikey`, `es_basic`, `opensearch_basic`, `opensearch_sigv4` reserved). New: 6 options (+ `solr_basic`, `solr_apikey`). The cross-product `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE` map filters which auth kinds are valid for a given engine — Solr offers only `solr_basic`/`solr_apikey`. Decision needed: **no**.
- **`/healthz` `subsystems` block.** Current: probes the local Compose `elasticsearch` + `opensearch` containers. New: also probes the local Compose `solr` container. Decision needed: **no** — additive.
- **UBI on-ramp engine-aware nudge.** Current: per-engine copy for ES + OpenSearch. New: adds a Solr branch with `solr.UBIComponent` enablement instructions. Decision needed: **no** — content-only extension.

## 3) Scope

### In scope

- `SolrAdapter` per [`adapters.md` §"SolrAdapter (MVP2)"](../../../../01_architecture/adapters.md), in new file `backend/app/adapters/solr.py`, implementing every method on the live `SearchAdapter` Protocol (`backend/app/adapters/protocol.py`): `health_check`, `list_targets`, `get_schema`, `list_query_parsers`, `render`, `search_batch`, `explain`, `get_document`, `list_documents`. The idea named 7 methods; the live Protocol has 10 (the two `get_document`/`list_documents` added by `feat_index_document_browser` must also be implemented for parity — otherwise the document-browser UI breaks the moment a Solr cluster is selected).
- Solr 9.x + 10.x support. Both SolrCloud (CollectionsAdmin) and standalone (CoresAdmin) modes. Selected automatically by the capability probe at construction.
- Auth flows `solr_basic` (HTTP Basic) and `solr_apikey` (Solr 9+ JWT through `security.json` `JWTAuthPlugin`). PKI auth is internal-only and not exposed.
- Capability probe at adapter construction: detects Solr version, SolrCloud-vs-standalone, presence of `solr.UBIComponent`, presence of the `ltr` module. Persists the result to `clusters.engine_config` JSONB for the search-space validator to consult.
- Cross-engine parameter map extension: the third column ("Solr `edismax`") in [`adapters.md` §"Cross-engine parameter naming"](../../../../01_architecture/adapters.md) becomes real implementation (today it's architectural reference). Includes the richer `mm` arithmetic syntax handling and the `bf`-vs-`boost` additive/multiplicative split driven by `boost_fn.combine`.
- LTR rescoring (consume-only): the unified `rerank_model: {id, top_k}` parameter renders to Solr `rq={!ltr model=${id} reRankDocs=${top_k}}`, applying a pre-existing `MultipleAdditiveTreesModel` loaded via Solr's `/schema/model-store`. **Training is explicitly out of scope** — see backlog.
- Templates under `samples/templates/solr/` as Jinja templates that emit Solr request-parameter dicts. Mirrors the locked `samples/templates/<engine>/` root (decision §19; preflight flagged this; see also `chore_template_library_expansion`).
- One Alembic migration extending `clusters.engine_type` CHECK to include `'solr'` and `clusters.auth_kind` CHECK to include `'solr_basic'` + `'solr_apikey'`. Reversible `downgrade()` per CLAUDE.md Absolute Rule #5. Round-trip verified.
- New Compose service `solr` (Apache 2.0 image, `solr:10`) bound to `127.0.0.1:8983`. Mirrors `elasticsearch` and `opensearch` service shape (named volume, healthcheck, `*_FILE`-mounted credentials). Optional admin credentials via `SOLR_ADMIN_USERNAME_FILE` + `SOLR_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE` (Compose-only; never bare env per Absolute Rule #2).
- Sample data: `products` collection on Compose Solr seeded from existing [`samples/products.json`](../../../../../samples/products.json) (same dataset MVP1 uses for ES).
- Frontend additions:
  - Extend [`ui/src/lib/enums.ts:40`](../../../../../ui/src/lib/enums.ts#L40) `ENGINE_TYPE_VALUES` with `'solr'`.
  - Extend [`ui/src/lib/enums.ts:44`](../../../../../ui/src/lib/enums.ts#L44) `AUTH_KIND_VALUES` with `'solr_basic'`, `'solr_apikey'`.
  - Extend the cluster-registration form ([`ui/src/components/clusters/register-cluster-modal.tsx`](../../../../../ui/src/components/clusters/register-cluster-modal.tsx)) with Solr auth help-text branches.
  - Add a Solr engine badge to cluster cards / study headers (small Apache Solr SVG; place under `ui/src/components/clusters/engine-badge.tsx` or extend the existing badge component).
  - Extend [`ui/src/components/clusters/ubi-onramp-nudge.tsx`](../../../../../ui/src/components/clusters/ubi-onramp-nudge.tsx) per-engine branch to include Solr's `solr.UBIComponent` enablement steps.
- Tests across all four layers (unit, integration, contract, E2E) per §14.
- Operator-facing documentation:
  - New runbook: `docs/03_runbooks/solr-cluster-registration.md`.
  - New tutorial Step 0 Path C in [`docs/08_guides/tutorial-first-study.md`](../../../../../docs/08_guides/tutorial-first-study.md): "Run the tutorial against Solr instead of Elasticsearch."
  - Update `docs/03_runbooks/ubi-judgment-generation.md` with a Solr section (enable `<searchComponent class="solr.UBIComponent">` in `solrconfig.xml`; route search requests through it).

### Out of scope

- **LTR training.** Consume-only is in scope; training a `MultipleAdditiveTreesModel` (XGBoost-compatible) is backlog (cross-engine training is a Path A v2 capability per [`adapters.md` §"Reserved for later releases"](../../../../01_architecture/adapters.md)).
- **Solr 8.x or earlier.** `edismax` and the Schema API are stable across 9 and 10; older versions are explicitly unsupported. Reject at registration with `ENGINE_VERSION_NOT_SUPPORTED` (existing pattern from `ElasticAdapter`).
- **Solr PKI authentication.** PKI is internal-to-Solr (used by SolrCloud node-to-node). RelyLoop is an external client; supports only `solr_basic` and `solr_apikey`.
- **`HTTPX_POOL_LIMITS` settings-level tunable.** Locked decision §19 — rely on the inline `httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=Timeout(10.0, connect=2.0))` defaults the `ElasticAdapter` uses today (line 124). Introduce a settings-level tunable later if integration tests show contention.
- **Hybrid UBI converter changes.** `feat_ubi_judgments` shipped the hybrid UBI+LLM converter; it works on Solr unchanged the moment this adapter lands. No converter modifications in this feature.
- **Audit log emission.** Per CLAUDE.md and `data-model.md`, `audit_log` lands at MVP3. This feature's cluster-write paths (already engine-agnostic on the `clusters` table) don't add new mutations beyond what the existing CRUD already does; instrumentation is N/A.
- **JSON Request API for multi-query.** Solr's JSON Request API allows multi-query but is awkward and undertested across 9.x and 10.x. Use parallel `/select` requests; revisit if a future operator profile shows we need multi-query.
- **AWS Solr deployments (AWS-managed search clusters).** Out of scope; same posture as `opensearch_sigv4` (reserved until production-stack adopters arrive).
- **Multi-Git provider abstraction.** GitLab/Bitbucket remain backlog regardless of engine count.

### API convention check

Follows [`api-conventions.md`](../../../../01_architecture/api-conventions.md):

- **Endpoint prefix:** business endpoints under `/api/v1/<resource>`. All existing cluster endpoints (`POST/GET/DELETE /api/v1/clusters`, `GET /api/v1/clusters/{id}/schema`, `POST /api/v1/clusters/{id}/run_query`, `GET /api/v1/clusters/{id}/ubi-readiness`) are engine-agnostic and need no new routes for Solr — they pivot internally via the adapter dispatch.
- **Router namespace:** [`backend/app/api/v1/clusters.py`](../../../../../backend/app/api/v1/clusters.py) (no new router file).
- **Error envelope:** structured `{ "detail": { "error_code", "message", "retryable" } }` per api-conventions.md. Solr-specific failures translate to the same router-side codes as ES (`CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE`, `TARGET_NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_QUERY_DSL`, `QUERY_TIMEOUT`, `TARGETS_FORBIDDEN`).
- **Auth error shape:** N/A (MVP1–MVP3 no auth surface).
- **New error codes introduced by this feature:** see §8.5. The only new code is `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` (Solr-specific, surfaced when an LTR rescore references a model id that's not in the cluster's `/schema/model-store`).

### Phase boundaries

**Single-phase.** All 10 stories (A1–A10 per `mvp2-overview.md`) ship in one PR. Rationale: the adapter is meaningless without (a) the migration that allows `engine_type=solr` to pass DB CHECK, (b) the Compose service that the integration tests run against, (c) the frontend allowlist extension that lets operators select Solr in the form. Splitting any of these into a follow-up phase creates a half-shipped state where the backend accepts Solr but the UI rejects it (or vice versa). The release-size estimate (~2–3 engineer-weeks solo) is small enough to ship atomically.

## 4) Product principles and constraints

- **The adapter Protocol is the engine boundary** (CLAUDE.md Absolute Rule #4). Every method on `SearchAdapter` that ES + OpenSearch implement, Solr must implement with the same signature and the same exception contract. No engine-specific code outside `backend/app/adapters/`.
- **Hot-path = `search_batch` only.** Parallel `/select` requests during a study trial; every other method (`health_check`, `get_schema`, `list_targets`, `render`, `explain`, `get_document`, `list_documents`) is define-time or debug-time. Per-`/select` parallelism is bounded by the inline `httpx.AsyncClient` default (1024 connections, no explicit `Limits`); locked decision §19.
- **Unified parameter vocabulary, native rendering.** Templates use unified names (`field_boosts`, `min_should_match`, `boost_fn`, `rerank_model`, etc.); the SolrAdapter's `render` pivots them to native Solr request-params (`qf`, `mm`, `bf`/`boost`, `rq={!ltr ...}`). The cross-engine parameter map in [`adapters.md`](../../../../01_architecture/adapters.md) is the contract.
- **Capability probe before validation, not after.** On adapter construction the probe writes Solr-discovered capabilities (UBI present? LTR module present? SolrCloud or standalone?) to `clusters.engine_config`. The search-space validator consults `engine_config` before accepting a study definition; studies that reference parameters the cluster can't honor are rejected before any trial runs.
- **Secrets via mounted files** (CLAUDE.md Absolute Rule #2). Compose-side admin credentials use `*_FILE` env vars; per-cluster auth uses `credentials_ref` keying into `cluster_credentials.yaml`. Never bare env.
- **Reversible migration, round-trip verified** (Absolute Rule #5). The CHECK-constraint migration includes a `downgrade()` that restores the original allowlists; verified with `alembic upgrade head && alembic downgrade -1 && alembic upgrade head` before merge.

### Anti-patterns

- **Do not** create a second `SolrAdapter`-flavored class hierarchy that re-derives the cross-engine vocabulary. The adapter consumes the unified vocabulary from `protocol.py` and pivots to native Solr — same contract as `ElasticAdapter`.
- **Do not** call Solr from any non-adapter module. If a service ever imports `httpx` to hit `/select` directly, the abstraction has leaked. Fix the Protocol or extend the adapter; never short-circuit.
- **Do not** add a bare `HTTPX_POOL_LIMITS` env var to ship parallel `/select`. Locked decision §19 — use the inline `httpx.AsyncClient` defaults; introduce a tunable only if integration tests show contention. Mirrors the ES adapter posture.
- **Do not** silently swallow Solr 5xx as "empty results" in the hot path with `strict_errors=False`. The Optuna trial runner does treat per-query failures as empty hits (so the trial records as failed without aborting the batch) — match that behavior exactly. The `run_query` debug path (`strict_errors=True`) raises `InvalidQueryDSLError` / `ClusterUnreachableError` like the ES side.
- **Do not** inline Solr-specific dropdown options in the React form. Every option flows through `enums.ts` (Enumerated Value Contract Discipline; CLAUDE.md form-select-discipline test will catch a regression).
- **Do not** ship a Solr UBI integration that depends on the operator pre-configuring `solr.UBIComponent`. The integration tests must bootstrap a fresh `solrconfig.xml` snippet (or use a pre-baked Solr config dir) so green tests imply the documented runbook works on a clean cluster.

## 5) Assumptions and dependencies

- **Dependency: MVP1 shipped.** `ElasticAdapter`, `SearchAdapter` Protocol, study lifecycle, judgment lists, PR worker. **Status:** all shipped. **Risk if missing:** N/A.
- **Dependency: `feat_ubi_judgments` shipped (PR #317, 2026-05-29).** `UbiReader`, `ubi_readiness` classifier, `POST /api/v1/judgments/generate-from-ubi`, hybrid UBI+LLM converter, frontend method picker + on-ramp nudge. The UBI surface is engine-agnostic by design; Solr just extends the engine_type set. **Status:** shipped. **Risk if missing:** would block the "every UBI path extends to Solr from day one" outcome.
- **Dependency: Apache Solr Apache 2.0 Docker image.** `solr:10` (official). Pinned to a minor version (e.g., `solr:10.0.0`) for deterministic CI; documented in `docker-compose.yml` and the runbook.
- **Dependency: `feat_demo_ubi_study_comparison` Phase 1 shipped (PR #320, 2026-05-30).** Established [`samples/ubi_index_mappings.json`](../../../../../samples/ubi_index_mappings.json) as the canonical UBI index mapping; the Solr integration tests reuse it for collection bootstrap. **Status:** shipped. **Risk if missing:** would require this feature to define the UBI mapping fixture; minor.
- **httpx async client.** Adapter uses one shared `httpx.AsyncClient` per cluster instance, same pattern as `ElasticAdapter` (line 124). `aclose()` called when the adapter goes out of scope.
- **No external services beyond the cluster the adapter is talking to.** Adapter is self-contained within the API process; does not enqueue jobs. UBI reading happens via the existing `UbiReader` which calls `search_batch` on this adapter.

## 6) Actors and roles

- **Primary actor:** Relevance Engineer (registers Solr clusters, runs studies, generates judgments).
- **Role model:** N/A — single-tenant install, no auth surface through MVP3.
- **Permission boundaries:** N/A.

### Authorization

**N/A — single-tenant install, no auth surface** (per [`docs/01_architecture/tech-stack.md` §"Canonical release matrix"](../../../../01_architecture/tech-stack.md)).

### Audit-event instrumentation matrix

**N/A — `audit_log` table lands at MVP3** (per [`data-model.md` §"Forthcoming: audit_log"](../../../../01_architecture/data-model.md)). This feature adds no new mutation endpoints; it extends the existing `POST /api/v1/clusters` allowlist to accept `engine_type=solr`. Cluster create/delete instrumentation will be added at MVP3 across all three engines in one pass.

## 7) Functional requirements

### FR-1: SolrAdapter implements every SearchAdapter Protocol method

- The system **MUST** implement `SolrAdapter` in `backend/app/adapters/solr.py` satisfying every method on the `SearchAdapter` Protocol at [`backend/app/adapters/protocol.py`](../../../../../backend/app/adapters/protocol.py): `health_check`, `list_targets`, `get_schema`, `list_query_parsers`, `render`, `search_batch`, `explain`, `get_document`, `list_documents`.
- The system **MUST** pass `isinstance(adapter, SearchAdapter)` (runtime-checkable Protocol).
- The system **MUST** raise the same exception classes as `ElasticAdapter` (`ClusterUnreachableError`, `TargetNotFoundError`, `TargetsForbiddenError`, `InvalidQueryDSLError`, `QueryTimeoutError`) so the router error-mapping is engine-agnostic. **Render-time unsupported-parameter handling:** when a unified parameter has no Solr equivalent (e.g., `fuzziness`), the SolrAdapter raises `InvalidQueryDSLError("unified parameter 'fuzziness' has no Solr edismax equivalent; use the '~' operator in the query body")` — reuses the existing exception class rather than introducing a new one, keeping the router contract engine-agnostic. The search-space validator (study-create path) does a pre-flight render against an empty target to catch these before any trial runs; this matches the ES adapter's pattern of failing fast at study-create.

#### Per-method Solr-specific notes

| Method | Solr endpoint / behavior |
|---|---|
| `health_check` | `GET /solr/admin/info/system` → parse `lucene.solr-spec-version` for `HealthStatus.version`; map cluster reachability to `green` / `yellow` / `red` / `unreachable`. Cached with the same TTL ElasticAdapter uses (30s — see `health_cache.py`). Auth failure → `unreachable` with `error` field naming the 401/403. |
| `list_query_parsers` | Returns `["edismax", "dismax", "lucene"]` for all supported Solr versions (these three parsers are stable in core Solr 9 + 10). Pure-Python — no HTTP call. Mirrors ElasticAdapter's static return of `["query_string", "simple_query_string"]`. |
| `list_targets` | See FR-7. |
| `get_schema` | See FR-6. |
| `render` | See FR-4. |
| `search_batch` | See FR-5. |
| `explain` | See FR-8. |
| `get_document` / `list_documents` | See FR-9. |

### FR-2: SolrAdapter supports Solr 9.x and 10.x; SolrCloud + standalone

- The system **MUST** support Apache Solr 9.x (≥ 9.0) and 10.x. Solr 8.x and earlier **MUST** be rejected at registration with `CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE` (503) and a message clearly naming the version mismatch — same pattern as ES <8.11 (existing precedent in `infra_adapter_elastic/feature_spec.md` §11 edge flows; no new error code needed). The Solr adapter's `health_check` returns `status=unreachable` + `error="Solr 9 or later required (cluster reports 8.11.4)"`; the router maps to 503 + `CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE`.
- The system **MUST** auto-detect SolrCloud vs standalone (capability probe), and use `CoresAdmin` (standalone) or `CollectionsAdmin` (SolrCloud) for `list_targets`.
- **Capability probe persistence is owned by the service layer, not the adapter constructor.** Adapter construction is a pure factory call (no DB session). The cluster-registration service path is: (1) `build_adapter(cluster)` constructs the adapter (no probe yet), (2) the service calls a new explicit method `adapter.probe_capabilities()` returning a pure-Python `ProbeResult` Pydantic model, (3) the service writes the result to `clusters.engine_config` JSONB **inside the same transaction** as the cluster row INSERT/UPDATE, before `db.commit()`. Atomicity: a failed probe rolls back the cluster row (the operator gets `CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE` and no half-registered row exists). Idempotency: `POST /api/v1/clusters/{id}/reprobe` (new endpoint, see §8.1) re-runs the probe in its own transaction; concurrent calls coalesce on a row-level lock.
- **Probe contents** (the `ProbeResult` Pydantic model): `version: str`, `mode: Literal["cloud", "standalone"]`, `ubi_component_present: bool`, `ltr_module_present: bool`, `ltr_models: list[str]`, `unique_key_per_target: dict[str, str]` (target → uniqueKey field name from Solr's `/schema/uniquekey`; **enumerated at probe time** by calling `list_targets` first then `/schema/uniquekey` per target — see FR-9 for the post-registration new-target handling rule).
- The system **MUST** test all four probe-write paths (POST /clusters create, POST /reprobe, seed-clusters idempotent rerun, edge case where probe partially succeeds — see §14 integration tests).

### FR-3: Auth flows `solr_basic` and `solr_apikey`

- The system **MUST** implement `solr_basic` (HTTP Basic; YAML credential file `{username, password}`). Wire header: `Authorization: Basic <base64(username:password)>` — same construction `ElasticAdapter._build_auth_headers` uses for `es_basic` (line 124 area). Credentials are resolved at adapter construction and cached for the adapter's lifetime; rotation requires re-registration or a new adapter instance (matches ES behavior).
- The system **MUST** implement `solr_apikey` (Solr 9+ JWT through `JWTAuthPlugin`). Credential file: YAML `{jwt_token}`. Wire header: `Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>` (the standard JWT header `JWTAuthPlugin` accepts). **`refresh_url` is OUT OF SCOPE for MVP2** — the credential file may carry it as a comment/metadata field but the adapter does not act on it. Rotation is operator-driven: update the mounted file + call `POST /api/v1/clusters/{id}/reprobe` to validate. Re-open as a follow-up if rotation friction becomes real.
- **Mandatory: relocate the engine/auth allowlists out of `elastic.py`.** Today `SUPPORTED_AUTH_KINDS` (line 70), `RESERVED_AUTH_KINDS` (line 73), and `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE` (line 79) live in `backend/app/adapters/elastic.py`. With a SolrAdapter landing, keeping the global allowlists in an ES-specific module forces non-ES code to import ElasticAdapter internals. **Move them to a neutral module `backend/app/adapters/registry.py`** (new file in this feature), re-export from `elastic.py` for one release as deprecated aliases to avoid churn on downstream imports. `SolrAdapter` imports from `registry.py`. Frontend `enums.ts` source-of-truth comments cite `registry.py`, not `elastic.py`.
- The system **MUST** extend the relocated `SUPPORTED_AUTH_KINDS` to include `solr_basic` + `solr_apikey`.
- The system **MUST** extend the relocated `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE` with `"solr": frozenset({"solr_basic", "solr_apikey"})` so cluster registration rejects mismatched pairings with 400 `AUTH_KIND_NOT_SUPPORTED` (e.g., `engine_type=solr` + `auth_kind=es_apikey` rejects).
- The system **MUST** raise a typed error (`CredentialFileInvalidError` → 400 `CREDENTIALS_INVALID`, existing pattern) at probe time if the credential file is malformed (missing `username`/`password` for `solr_basic`, missing `jwt_token` for `solr_apikey`, file not readable, etc.). The probe (FR-2) drives this — registration aborts atomically with the cluster row never committed.
- **Compose-side bootstrap for `solr_basic` testing.** Solr's `security.json` is a static file and cannot directly resolve Docker secret-file references (it expects a literal `BasicAuthPlugin` credentials block with bcrypt-hashed password). The Compose bootstrap is therefore two-step (avoiding the cycle-2 contradiction GPT-5.5 caught):
  1. **Entrypoint script** `docker/solr/bootstrap-security.sh` (new) runs before `solr-foreground`: reads `SOLR_ADMIN_USERNAME_FILE` + `SOLR_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE`, generates a `security.json` at `/var/solr/data/security.json` with `BasicAuthPlugin` configured for the resolved credentials. Idempotent — skips regeneration if `security.json` already exists AND the embedded credential hashes match. The bcrypt hash is computed at bootstrap time (Solr ships `bin/solr auth enable-basic-auth` which does this; the script invokes that).
  2. **`security.json` configures `/admin/info/system` as an anonymous-allowlist endpoint** (`authorization.permissions` includes a public rule for `/admin/info/system`), so the unauthenticated healthcheck `curl -fsS http://localhost:8983/solr/admin/info/system` keeps working. All other admin/data endpoints require BasicAuth — `/admin/collections`, `/admin/cores`, `/<target>/select`, `/<target>/schema/*`, `/schema/model-store`, etc.
  3. Integration tests authenticate via the same secret files. The runbook documents both the bootstrap script and the manual `security.json` shape for operators not using Compose.

### FR-4: `render` for `edismax` / `dismax` / `lucene` parsers

- The system **MUST** implement `render(template, params, query_text)` producing a Solr request-parameter dict (key→value, later URL-encoded by `_request`). Supports `edismax` (primary), `dismax`, and `lucene` parsers; the parser is chosen by the template.
- The system **MUST** pivot the unified parameter vocabulary to native Solr per [`adapters.md` §"Cross-engine parameter naming"](../../../../01_architecture/adapters.md):
  - `field_boosts: {f: w}` → `qf=f^w` (space-joined for multiple fields).
  - `phrase_field_boosts` → `pf`.
  - `tie_breaker` → `tie`.
  - `min_should_match` → `mm` (accepts unified `int | float | str`; validates against Solr's `mm` arithmetic syntax server-side).
  - `slop` → `ps`.
  - `boost_fn: {field, type, params, combine: "add"|"multiply"}` → `bf=…` (when `combine=add`) or `boost=…` (when `combine=multiply`).
  - `rerank_model: {id, top_k}` → `rq={!ltr model=${id} reRankDocs=${top_k}}`.
- The system **MUST** raise `InvalidQueryDSLError` at render time when a unified parameter has no Solr equivalent (e.g., `fuzziness`, which Solr handles via the `~` operator in the query parser, not as a request-param). Message format: `"unified parameter '<name>' has no Solr edismax equivalent; use the '~' operator in the query body"`. No new exception class — reuses the existing `InvalidQueryDSLError` per FR-1 mapping decision (GPT-5.5 cycle 1 F3; do not introduce `UnsupportedParameter` as a public adapter exception).
- The system **MUST** load Jinja templates from `samples/templates/solr/` (locked path per §19).

### FR-5: `search_batch` via parallel `/select` requests

- The system **MUST** implement `search_batch(target, queries, top_k, *, request_id?, strict_errors=False, timeout?)` by issuing parallel `/select` requests against the target collection/core (one request per `NativeQuery`), gathered via `asyncio.gather`.
- The system **MUST** preserve `query_id` mapping between input order and the response dict.
- The system **MUST** propagate `request_id` (when provided) as an HTTP header for Solr-side log correlation (Solr supports `X-Request-Id` natively via the request-log component; verify exact header at impl time).
- The system **MUST** honor `strict_errors=True` by raising `InvalidQueryDSLError` on per-query 400-class failures and `ClusterUnreachableError` on connection failures / 5xx (matches `ElasticAdapter` `_request` behavior).
- The system **MUST** honor `strict_errors=False` (Optuna hot path) by yielding empty `[]` for failed queries so the trial records as failed without aborting the batch.
- The system **MUST** honor the `timeout` override (per the existing per-trial-timeout convention shipped by `infra_per_trial_timeout`).
- The system **MUST NOT** introduce a settings-level `HTTPX_POOL_LIMITS` knob in MVP2; use the inline `httpx.AsyncClient` default (locked decision §19).

### FR-6: `get_schema` via Solr Schema API

- The system **MUST** implement `get_schema(target)` returning `Schema(name=target, fields=[FieldSpec(...)])` derived from Solr's Schema API (`/<target>/schema/fields`, `/<target>/schema/dynamicfields`, `/<target>/schema/fieldtypes`).
- The system **MUST** populate `FieldSpec.type` with the Solr field-type label (`text_general`, `string`, `pfloat`, `boolean`, `pdate`, etc.) and `FieldSpec.analyzer` with the per-field analyzer name when discoverable.
- The system **MUST** raise `TargetNotFoundError` on 404 from the Schema API.

### FR-7: `list_targets` via CoresAdmin or CollectionsAdmin

- The system **MUST** implement `list_targets(*, request_id?, target_filter?)` using the API the capability probe chose:
  - **Standalone Solr:** `GET /solr/admin/cores?action=STATUS` → parse `status.<core>.name`.
  - **SolrCloud:** `GET /solr/admin/collections?action=LIST` → parse `collections[]`.
- The system **MUST** apply the `target_filter` glob per the existing convention (`fnmatch.fnmatchcase`, system-target exclusion FIRST then glob filter); same semantics as ES (`elastic.py` `list_targets`).
- The system **MUST** raise `TargetsForbiddenError` on 401/403 (Solr's `BasicAuthPlugin` / `JWTAuthPlugin` denying the admin call) so the frontend auto-engages manual-mode target entry.
- The system **MUST** raise `ClusterUnreachableError` on connection failures / 5xx.

### FR-8: `explain` via `debugQuery=true&debug=results`

- The system **MUST** implement `explain(target, query, doc_id)` by issuing the `query` as `/select` with `debugQuery=true&debug=results&fq=id:<doc_id>` and parsing the `debug.explain` block.
- The system **MUST** convert the Solr explain tree into the unified `ExplainTree` shape (`doc_id`, `matched`, `value`, `description`, `details[]`) — same recursive shape as the ES `_explain` parser.

### FR-9: `get_document` and `list_documents` (document-browser parity)

- The system **MUST** implement `get_document(target, doc_id)` via Solr's **RealTime Get** endpoint: `GET /<target>/get?id=<escaped_doc_id>`. RealTime Get works against the per-target uniqueKey directly (no Lucene query parsing; no query escaping needed beyond URL-encoding); avoids the `q=id:<value>` pitfall when the value contains Lucene metacharacters. Returns `None` when Solr's `doc` field is null in the response; raises `TargetNotFoundError` on 404 for the target.
- The system **MUST** discover the per-target uniqueKey at first call via `GET /<target>/schema/uniquekey` and cache it in `engine_config.unique_key_per_target` (populated lazily on first `get_document`/`list_documents` call per target, persisted via the `reprobe` path). Solr's uniqueKey defaults to `id` but is configurable per collection — assuming `id` breaks against any operator's customized schema. The cached value is used to construct `cursorMark` sort and RealTime Get URLs.
- The system **MUST** implement `list_documents(target, *, search_after?, limit=25, fields?)` using Solr's cursor-based deep paging: `q=*:*&sort=<uniqueKey> asc&rows=<limit>&cursorMark=<token>`. The token comes from Solr's `nextCursorMark` response field (opaque, not per-hit); the initial call uses `cursorMark=*`. **No `limit + 1` overfetch on Solr** — Solr computes `nextCursorMark` based on ALL returned rows, so a `limit + 1` overfetch with the trailing entry stripped as a sentinel would cause the next page to skip exactly the stripped document (a real data-loss bug GPT-5.5 cycle 2 caught). Use `rows=<limit>` exactly. **Token-encoding bridge for the unified Protocol:** the Solr `nextCursorMark` doesn't fit the per-hit `AdapterDocumentHit.sort` shape (Elastic's `search_after` semantics). The SolrAdapter populates `AdapterDocumentHit.sort=[]` on every hit (no per-hit sort needed for cursorMark) AND populates a new optional `DocumentPage.next_cursor_token: str | None` field. **Terminal-condition rule (GPT-5.5 cycle 3 C3-F1):** Solr returns `nextCursorMark` even on the last page; the last page's `nextCursorMark` equals the `cursorMark` sent in the request. The SolrAdapter MUST set `next_cursor_token = nextCursorMark` ONLY when `nextCursorMark != current_cursorMark`; otherwise set `next_cursor_token = None`. This makes `has_more = next_cursor_token is not None` correct on the terminal page (otherwise the document browser would loop or report has_more=true forever). The router-side cursor encoder reads `next_cursor_token` when present (Solr) and falls back to the trailing hit's `sort` value when absent (ES). On the next page's call the router decodes its opaque cursor back to either a `search_after` list (ES) or a Solr `cursorMark` string; both flow through the adapter via a single shared parameter slot. **The unified Protocol grows a new optional field on `DocumentPage`** — additive change, ES adapter sets it to `None`, no impact on existing call sites.
- **`engine_config.unique_key_per_target` is populated at registration/reprobe time only** (NOT lazily by the adapter — would violate the service-layer-only write invariant). The probe enumerates current targets via `list_targets` and calls `/schema/uniquekey` for each. New targets created on the cluster after registration trigger an in-memory uniqueKey fetch by the adapter at first `get_document`/`list_documents` call (cached for the adapter's lifetime, NOT persisted); operators run `POST /reprobe` to persist the new target's uniqueKey to `engine_config`. Spec §8.2 and §9 enumerate `unique_key_per_target: {target_name: str}` as a required `engine_config` field for Solr clusters.
- The system **MUST** raise `TargetNotFoundError` on 404, `TargetsForbiddenError` on 401/403, `ClusterUnreachableError` on connection failures / 5xx after retry.

### FR-10: LTR rescoring (consume-only)

- The system **MUST** render the unified `rerank_model: {id, top_k}` parameter to Solr `rq={!ltr model=${id} reRankDocs=${top_k}}` in `render` (see FR-4) and `search_batch` (the rendered query body is opaque to `search_batch`).
- The system **MUST** raise a new `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` error (400) when a study's search space references a `rerank_model.id` that doesn't exist in the cluster's `/schema/model-store`. The error message lists the missing id and the available model ids. The capability probe pre-loads the model list to fail fast at registration where possible.
- The system **MUST NOT** train models; training is backlog. The model file must exist on the Solr side before a study using it can run.

### FR-11: Compose service, sample data, runbook, tutorial Path C

- The system **MUST** add a `solr` service to `docker-compose.yml` pinned to **`solr:10.0`** (the latest minor at spec time — bump in the runbook when a new minor lands, not in the spec). Port `127.0.0.1:8983`, named volume for `/var/solr`, admin credentials via `SOLR_ADMIN_USERNAME_FILE` + `SOLR_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE` mounted secrets per CLAUDE.md Absolute Rule #2. **BasicAuth setup follows the FR-3 bootstrap-script flow** — `docker/solr/bootstrap-security.sh` (new) runs at container startup, reads the `*_FILE` secrets, calls `bin/solr auth enable-basic-auth` to generate a hashed `security.json` at `/var/solr/data/security.json`. **Only the script is checked into the repo, not a credential-bearing static `security.json`** (avoids committing dev secrets to git; the file is generated at runtime). See FR-3 for the two-step bootstrap detail.
- **Healthcheck:** `curl -fsS http://localhost:8983/solr/admin/info/system` — works against a fresh container before any core/collection exists (verified empirically; `/solr/admin/ping` is core-scoped and 404s on an empty standalone node). 30s interval, 5s timeout, 5 retries.
- The system **MUST** seed a `products` collection on first `make up` from existing [`samples/products.json`](../../../../../samples/products.json) (same dataset MVP1 uses for ES).
- The system **MUST** add new runbook `docs/03_runbooks/solr-cluster-registration.md` covering: register a Solr cluster via the UI; configure `edismax` defaults; enable `<searchComponent class="solr.UBIComponent">` in `solrconfig.xml`; upload an LTR model to `/schema/model-store`.
- The system **MUST** add a Step 0 Path C section to [`docs/08_guides/tutorial-first-study.md`](../../../../../docs/08_guides/tutorial-first-study.md) walking the operator through running the full tutorial against Solr instead of Elasticsearch.

### FR-12a: `/healthz` subsystems extension for the local Compose Solr

- The system **MUST** add a `subsystems.solr` field to the `/healthz` response **whenever the local Compose Solr is configured** (detected by presence of `SOLR_HOST` env var — config is the gate, not reachability). Shape: `{status: "green"|"yellow"|"red"|"unreachable", version?: string}`, matching the existing `subsystems.elasticsearch` block. Configured-but-unreachable (connection-refused, timeout, 5xx) reports as `subsystems.solr.status="unreachable"` — the field is ALWAYS present when configured, even when Solr is down (matches AC-15's "stopped Compose service still shows the field" expectation). Only omit the field entirely when Solr is truly not configured (no `SOLR_HOST`).
- The system **MUST** respect the existing `/healthz` 200ms per-subsystem timeout (CLAUDE.md absolute rule #11); Solr probe runs via `asyncio.wait_for(probe(), timeout=0.2)`. On TimeoutError, status is `unreachable`.
- The system **MUST NOT** fail the overall `/healthz` response when Solr is absent (production deployments without Solr stay green). Solr-down is reported as `subsystems.solr.status = "unreachable"`; only Postgres or other Always-Required subsystems can fail the overall endpoint.

### FR-12: Frontend allowlist + auth-form extension + Solr engine badge

- The system **MUST** extend `ENGINE_TYPE_VALUES` at [`ui/src/lib/enums.ts:40`](../../../../../ui/src/lib/enums.ts#L40) with `'solr'` (the Enumerated Value Contract Discipline source-of-truth comment must already cite the backend Literal).
- The system **MUST** extend `AUTH_KIND_VALUES` at [`ui/src/lib/enums.ts:44`](../../../../../ui/src/lib/enums.ts#L44) with `'solr_basic'` and `'solr_apikey'`.
- The system **MUST** add an engine-aware auth-help branch in [`ui/src/components/clusters/register-cluster-modal.tsx`](../../../../../ui/src/components/clusters/register-cluster-modal.tsx) describing each Solr auth flow.
- The system **MUST** render a Solr engine badge on cluster cards and study headers (Apache Solr SVG, accessible label).
- The system **MUST** extend [`ui/src/components/clusters/ubi-onramp-nudge.tsx`](../../../../../ui/src/components/clusters/ubi-onramp-nudge.tsx) with a Solr branch instructing the operator to enable `solr.UBIComponent` in `solrconfig.xml` and deep-linking the runbook.
- The system **MUST** preserve all existing `feat_ubi_judgments` Capability A–E behaviors (rung classification, sparse-data card, value-delta) on Solr clusters without code change — they're engine-agnostic.

## 8) API and data contract baseline

### 8.1 Endpoint surface

**One new endpoint** (`POST /api/v1/clusters/{id}/reprobe`); every other cluster endpoint is engine-agnostic and accepts `engine_type=solr` through the dispatch in [`backend/app/services/cluster.py`](../../../../../backend/app/services/cluster.py) (`build_adapter` line 325) once `SolrAdapter` is registered.

| Method | Path | New behavior on Solr |
|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/v1/clusters` | Accepts `engine_type=solr` + `auth_kind ∈ {solr_basic, solr_apikey}`. Capability probe (FR-2) runs in the same transaction; failure rolls back the row. |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/clusters` | Lists Solr clusters alongside ES + OpenSearch. |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/clusters/{id}` | Returns `engine_type=solr` + populated `engine_config` (`version`, `mode`, `ubi_component_present`, `ltr_module_present`, `ltr_models[]`, `unique_key_per_target`). |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/clusters/{id}/reprobe` | **NEW**. Re-runs the capability probe (FR-2) and updates `engine_config` in a new transaction. Engine-agnostic but most useful on Solr (LTR model uploads, UBI component enablement happen out-of-band). 202 on success with the updated cluster row; 503 `CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE` if probe fails. Concurrent calls coalesce on `SELECT … FOR UPDATE`. |
| `DELETE` | `/api/v1/clusters/{id}` | Soft-delete; engine-agnostic. |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/clusters/{id}/schema?target=<name>` | Routes through `SolrAdapter.get_schema`. |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/clusters/{id}/documents/{doc_id}?target=<name>` | Existing document-browser endpoint shipped by `feat_index_document_browser`. **Routes through `SolrAdapter.get_document`** (FR-9) which uses Solr's RealTime Get against the per-target uniqueKey. Errors mirror ES: 404 `TARGET_NOT_FOUND` (target missing), 404 `RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND` (target exists but doc_id has no match), 403 `TARGETS_FORBIDDEN` (auth denied), 503 `CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE`. |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/clusters/{id}/documents?target=<name>&cursor=<opaque>&limit=<n>&fields=<csv>` | Existing document-browser list endpoint. **Routes through `SolrAdapter.list_documents`** (FR-9) which paginates via Solr's `cursorMark`. The router's opaque cursor encoding wraps the SolrAdapter's adapter-level cursorMark; clients are oblivious — `cursor` parameter is engine-agnostic. |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/clusters/{id}/run_query` | Routes through `SolrAdapter.search_batch(strict_errors=True)`. `query_dsl` request body for Solr is a request-params dict (`{q, qf, mm, ...}`); the runbook documents the shape. |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/clusters/{id}/ubi-readiness` | Existing endpoint shipped by `feat_ubi_judgments`; works on Solr unchanged (the probe under the hood now also looks for `ubi_queries` / `ubi_events` collections on Solr). |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/judgments/generate-from-ubi` | Existing endpoint; works on Solr unchanged via the engine-agnostic `UbiReader`. |
| `GET` | `/healthz` | **Subsystems block extension.** Adds a `subsystems.solr` field probing the local Compose `solr` container (when present). Field shape mirrors the existing `subsystems.elasticsearch` block: `{status: "green"|"yellow"|"red"|"unreachable", version?: string}`. Optional in production deployments where `solr` isn't running — the probe respects a 200ms timeout (per CLAUDE.md `/healthz` rule); absence reports as `unreachable` rather than failing the whole endpoint. |

### 8.2 Contract rules

- `engine_type=solr` validates against the **Pydantic `EngineType` Literal** in `protocol.py` (extended in this feature) AND the **DB CHECK** on `clusters.engine_type` (extended in this feature's migration). Both layers must accept `'solr'`; either layer alone is insufficient.
- `auth_kind ∈ {solr_basic, solr_apikey}` paired with `engine_type=solr` validates against `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE`. Mismatched pairings (e.g., `engine_type=solr` + `auth_kind=es_apikey`) reject with 400 `AUTH_KIND_NOT_SUPPORTED` at registration.
- `clusters.engine_config` for Solr clusters carries `{version: str, mode: "cloud"|"standalone", ubi_component_present: bool, ltr_module_present: bool, ltr_models: [id, ...], unique_key_per_target: {target: uniqueKey_field}}` — written by the capability probe at registration; updated by `POST /reprobe`; read by the search-space validator before each study create AND by the SolrAdapter at `get_document`/`list_documents` time.
- Solr-specific failures use the existing error codes (`CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE`, `TARGET_NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_QUERY_DSL`, `QUERY_TIMEOUT`, `TARGETS_FORBIDDEN`). One new code added: `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` (see §8.5).

### 8.3 Response examples

`POST /api/v1/clusters` success (201) — Solr standalone:

```json
{
  "id": "01935e9d-1234-7000-8001-abcdef012345",
  "name": "local-solr",
  "engine_type": "solr",
  "environment": "dev",
  "base_url": "http://solr:8983",
  "auth_kind": "solr_basic",
  "engine_config": {
    "version": "10.0.0",
    "mode": "standalone",
    "ubi_component_present": true,
    "ltr_module_present": true,
    "ltr_models": [],
    "unique_key_per_target": {"products": "id"}
  },
  "notes": null,
  "created_at": "2026-05-30T15:00:00Z",
  "health_check": {"status": "green", "version": "10.0.0", "checked_at": "2026-05-30T15:00:00Z"}
}
```

Failure (400 — mismatched `auth_kind` for `engine_type=solr`):

```json
{
  "detail": {
    "error_code": "AUTH_KIND_NOT_SUPPORTED",
    "message": "auth_kind 'es_apikey' is not valid for engine_type 'solr'. Valid: solr_basic, solr_apikey.",
    "retryable": false
  }
}
```

Failure (400 — LTR model referenced in a study but not present in `/schema/model-store`):

```json
{
  "detail": {
    "error_code": "LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND",
    "message": "LTR model 'xgboost_v2' not present in cluster 'local-solr'. Available: ['xgboost_v1']. Upload the model to /schema/model-store before referencing it in a search space.",
    "retryable": false
  }
}
```

Failure (503 — Solr unreachable on registration):

```json
{
  "detail": {
    "error_code": "CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE",
    "message": "Cluster at http://solr:8983 did not respond within 5s",
    "retryable": true
  }
}
```

### 8.4 Enumerated value contracts

| Field | Accepted values (exact) | Backend source of truth | Frontend call site |
|---|---|---|---|
| `engine_type` | `elasticsearch`, `opensearch`, **`solr`** *(new)* | [`backend/app/adapters/protocol.py`](../../../../../backend/app/adapters/protocol.py) (`EngineType` `Literal[...]`) + DB CHECK at [`cluster.py:34`](../../../../../backend/app/db/models/cluster.py#L34) | [`ui/src/lib/enums.ts:40`](../../../../../ui/src/lib/enums.ts#L40) `ENGINE_TYPE_VALUES` → cluster-registration form |
| `auth_kind` | `es_apikey`, `es_basic`, `opensearch_basic`, `opensearch_sigv4`, **`solr_basic`** *(new)*, **`solr_apikey`** *(new)* | `backend/app/adapters/registry.py` (new in this feature) `SUPPORTED_AUTH_KINDS` + `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE` — the relocated single source of truth. The old `backend/app/adapters/elastic.py:70` `SUPPORTED_AUTH_KINDS` definition becomes a deprecated re-export for one release per FR-3. DB CHECK at [`cluster.py:42`](../../../../../backend/app/db/models/cluster.py#L42) | [`ui/src/lib/enums.ts:44`](../../../../../ui/src/lib/enums.ts#L44) `AUTH_KIND_VALUES` (source-of-truth comment must cite `registry.py` per FR-3) → cluster-registration form |
| `clusters.engine_config.mode` (Solr-only) | `cloud`, `standalone` | `backend/app/adapters/solr.py` (new constant `SOLR_MODE_VALUES`) | not user-facing (badge derives from this) |
| `health_check.status` | `green`, `yellow`, `red`, `unreachable` | `backend/app/adapters/protocol.py` `HealthStatus.status` Literal | cluster detail page |

**Note:** the `opensearch_sigv4` reserved kind is paired with `engine_type=opensearch` only (not `solr`); `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE` enforces.

### 8.5 Error code catalog

Only one new code introduced. All other Solr failure modes reuse the existing engine-agnostic codes.

| Code | HTTP Status | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` | 400 | A study's search space (or a `run_query` request) references a `rerank_model.id` that doesn't exist in the Solr cluster's `/schema/model-store`. `retryable: false`. Recovery: upload the model to Solr (operator runbook step) or remove the parameter from the search space. The capability probe lists available model ids in the error message and in `engine_config.ltr_models`. |

## 9) Data model and state transitions

### Modified table: `clusters`

- **Extend `clusters_engine_type_check` CHECK constraint** at [`cluster.py:33–36`](../../../../../backend/app/db/models/cluster.py#L33) from `engine_type IN ('elasticsearch', 'opensearch')` to `engine_type IN ('elasticsearch', 'opensearch', 'solr')`.
- **Extend `clusters_auth_kind_check` CHECK constraint** at [`cluster.py:41–44`](../../../../../backend/app/db/models/cluster.py#L41) from `auth_kind IN ('es_apikey', 'es_basic', 'opensearch_basic', 'opensearch_sigv4')` to `auth_kind IN ('es_apikey', 'es_basic', 'opensearch_basic', 'opensearch_sigv4', 'solr_basic', 'solr_apikey')`.

### Migration

- **One new migration file:** `migrations/versions/00XX_solr_engine_auth_check.py` (revision id assigned at impl time; ≤ 32 chars; sequential after the current Alembic head `0021_judgment_lists_generation_params` per state.md).
- **`upgrade()`:** `DROP CONSTRAINT` + `ADD CONSTRAINT` for both CHECKs with the new allowlists. Idempotency-guarded.
- **`downgrade()`:** restores the original CHECKs (must reject any Solr rows that would violate the old allowlist — surface as a clear error at `downgrade` time if Solr rows exist).
- **Round-trip verified** with `alembic upgrade head && alembic downgrade -1 && alembic upgrade head`.

### Required invariants

- `clusters.engine_config` for Solr clusters is non-null after registration and includes `version`, `mode`, `ubi_component_present`, `ltr_module_present`, `ltr_models`, **`unique_key_per_target`** (mapping `target_name → uniqueKey field`; populated at registration/reprobe; adapter-side in-memory cache for targets created post-registration) keys (Pydantic-validated at the API layer).
- `engine_type=solr` rows must pair with `auth_kind ∈ {solr_basic, solr_apikey}` (enforced by `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE` cross-product check at registration; DB CHECK enforces the basic allowlist but not the cross-product).
- The Solr adapter never writes to Solr — all writes happen via the existing config-repo PR flow (which RelyLoop's existing PR worker handles engine-agnostically).
- **Adapter constructors do not perform DB I/O.** Capability-probe persistence happens in the service layer (`backend/app/services/cluster.py`), in the same transaction as the cluster row INSERT/UPDATE.

### Protocol-shape change

This feature adds **one additive optional field** to `DocumentPage` in [`backend/app/adapters/protocol.py`](../../../../../backend/app/adapters/protocol.py):

```python
class DocumentPage(BaseModel):
    hits: list[AdapterDocumentHit]
    total: int
    next_cursor_token: str | None = None  # NEW — Solr nextCursorMark; None for ES/OpenSearch
```

The router-side cursor encoder reads `next_cursor_token` when populated (Solr `cursorMark` path) and falls back to the trailing `AdapterDocumentHit.sort` value when absent (ES `search_after` path). `has_more` is computed from `next_cursor_token is not None` for Solr; the existing `len(hits) > user_limit` overfetch test still works for ES. **The existing `feat_index_document_browser` router contract is unchanged** — the field is optional, defaults to `None`, and the encoder gracefully handles both code paths. ES adapter test suite expects no regression.

### State transitions

No new state machine. Cluster lifecycle is unchanged: `active` → `deleted` (soft-delete via `clusters.deleted_at`).

## 10) Security, privacy, and compliance

- **Threats:**
  1. Stored Solr admin credentials in `cluster_credentials.yaml` reveal cluster auth. **Mitigation:** `credentials_ref` is a key into the mounted secrets file (same model as ES); the DB never stores the credential value. Compose-side `SOLR_ADMIN_USERNAME_FILE`/`SOLR_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE` use the same `*_FILE`-mounted pattern.
  2. `run_query` lets the operator execute arbitrary Solr DSL — could be abused to deplete cluster resources. **Mitigation:** time-budget (5s default, 30s max per the existing `run_query` cap), top-K cap, single-tenant install.
  3. LTR model uploads are out-of-band (operator uses Solr's `/schema/model-store` directly); RelyLoop never proxies model uploads. **Mitigation:** `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` (FR-10) prevents studies from referencing non-existent models, but the operator is responsible for the model's correctness.
  4. JWT credential rotation (`solr_apikey`). **Mitigation:** the YAML credential file supports an optional `refresh_url` field; rotation is operator-driven (update the mounted file; next adapter call uses the new JWT). No DB write.
- **Secrets handling:** per CLAUDE.md Absolute Rule #2 — `*_FILE`-mounted secrets only. Bare env vars rejected.
- **Auditability:** N/A — no audit subsystem in MVP2 (lands MVP3).
- **Data retention:** Cluster rows are soft-deleted (existing convention); never auto-purged.

## 11) UX flows and edge cases

### Information architecture

This feature does not introduce a new UI page. All Solr-specific UI surfaces are extensions of existing components:

- **Cluster-registration modal** ([`register-cluster-modal.tsx`](../../../../../ui/src/components/clusters/register-cluster-modal.tsx)): the `engine_type` dropdown gains a third option labeled "Apache Solr"; selecting it filters the `auth_kind` dropdown to `solr_basic` / `solr_apikey` and renders a Solr-specific auth-help text panel.
- **Cluster cards + study headers**: render the Solr engine badge (Apache Solr SVG) alongside the existing ES + OpenSearch badges.
- **UBI on-ramp nudge** ([`ubi-onramp-nudge.tsx`](../../../../../ui/src/components/clusters/ubi-onramp-nudge.tsx)): adds a third per-engine branch with `solr.UBIComponent` enablement copy + deep-link to the new runbook.

### Tooltips and contextual help

| Element | Tooltip / help text | Trigger | Placement |
|---|---|---|---|
| `engine_type=solr` option | "Apache Solr 9 or 10. Standalone or SolrCloud auto-detected." | hover | inline below |
| `auth_kind=solr_basic` option | "HTTP Basic against Solr's `BasicAuthPlugin`. Configure in `security.json`." | hover | inline below |
| `auth_kind=solr_apikey` option | "Solr 9+ JWT through `JWTAuthPlugin`. The credential file holds the JWT token." | hover | inline below |
| Solr engine badge | "Apache Solr — Solr 10 (standalone)" / "Solr 10 (cloud)" — value derives from `engine_config.mode` | hover | tooltip on badge |
| `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` error inline | "Upload the model to Solr's /schema/model-store first. See the Solr cluster runbook." | always-on (error inline help text) | below the error |

All tooltip copy added in this feature must register with the existing glossary (`ui/src/lib/glossary.ts`) under a new section "Solr adapter" so the `feat_contextual_help` glossary guard catches drift.

### Primary flows

1. **Register a Solr cluster.** Operator opens the cluster-registration modal → selects `engine_type=Apache Solr` → form filters `auth_kind` to Solr options → operator picks `solr_basic` and provides `credentials_ref` (key into `cluster_credentials.yaml`) → POST `/api/v1/clusters` → adapter probes the cluster → `engine_config` populated → cluster appears in the list with the Solr badge.
2. **Run the tutorial against Solr (Step 0 Path C).** Operator follows the new tutorial section: `make up` brings up the `solr` Compose service alongside ES/OpenSearch; the seed script creates a `products` collection from `samples/products.json`; `make seed-clusters` registers `local-solr` alongside `local-es`/`local-opensearch`; the rest of the tutorial proceeds identically.
3. **Generate UBI judgments from a Solr cluster.** Operator opens the judgment-generation modal on a query set bound to a Solr cluster with `solr.UBIComponent` enabled → the existing UBI method picker (from `feat_ubi_judgments`) lists "UBI" / "LLM" / "Hybrid" → operator picks UBI → POST `/api/v1/judgments/generate-from-ubi` enqueues the existing Arq worker → `UbiReader.search_batch` runs against `SolrAdapter` without engine-specific branching → judgments created with `source='click'`.
4. **Use an LTR rescore in a study.** Operator uploads an `MultipleAdditiveTreesModel` to Solr's `/schema/model-store` (out-of-band; runbook documents the API call) → creates a study with `search_space.params.rerank_model = {id: "xgboost_v1", top_k: 100}` → POST validates the model exists via the cached `engine_config.ltr_models` → study runs; each trial includes the LTR rescore stage.

### Edge/error flows

- **Solr reachable but `solr.UBIComponent` not enabled.** `engine_config.ubi_component_present=false` → the UBI on-ramp nudge surfaces with Solr-specific enablement copy on the cluster-detail page and the judgment-generation modal. Operator runs the runbook steps; on next page load, the probe re-runs (or via an explicit "re-probe" action; out of scope here, deferred to a future capability if needed).
- **Solr `ltr` module not loaded.** `engine_config.ltr_module_present=false` → study create rejects with `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` if the search space references `rerank_model` (the message clarifies "the ltr module isn't loaded on this cluster — add `<lib dir="${solr.install.dir}/modules/ltr/lib" />` to `solrconfig.xml`").
- **SolrCloud collection deleted out-of-band.** First `search_batch` call returns `TargetNotFoundError` → router maps to 404 `TARGET_NOT_FOUND` per existing convention.
- **Solr 8.x cluster.** Capability probe detects version < 9.0 → registration rejects with `CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE` and message "Solr 9 or later required (cluster reports 8.11.4)". Same posture as ES < 8.11.
- **Engine + auth mismatch (`engine_type=solr` + `auth_kind=es_basic`).** Rejected with 400 `AUTH_KIND_NOT_SUPPORTED` at registration (cross-product check in `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE`).
- **`mm` syntax error in template.** Solr rejects with 400 → `search_batch(strict_errors=True)` raises `InvalidQueryDSLError` → router maps to 400 `INVALID_QUERY_DSL` with Solr's error message inline.

## 12) Given/When/Then acceptance criteria

### AC-1: Register a local Solr cluster

- Given the local Compose `solr` service is healthy on `127.0.0.1:8983`.
- When the operator POSTs `{name: "local-solr", engine_type: "solr", environment: "dev", base_url: "http://solr:8983", auth_kind: "solr_basic", credentials_ref: "local_solr"}` to `/api/v1/clusters`.
- Then the response is 201 with `engine_config.version` matching `solr:10`, `engine_config.mode = "standalone"`, `engine_config.ubi_component_present = true`, `engine_config.ltr_module_present = true`, `health_check.status = "green"`.

### AC-2: Reject mismatched auth kind

- Given a registration request with `engine_type: "solr"` and `auth_kind: "es_apikey"`.
- When the request lands.
- Then the response is 400 with `error_code: "AUTH_KIND_NOT_SUPPORTED"` and message containing `"valid: solr_basic, solr_apikey"`. No cluster row is created.

### AC-3: Inspect a Solr schema

- Given a `products` collection on `local-solr` with 1,000 docs.
- When the operator hits `GET /api/v1/clusters/<local-solr-id>/schema?target=products`.
- Then the response is 200 with `Schema(name="products", fields=[FieldSpec(name="title", type="text_general", ...), FieldSpec(name="price", type="pfloat", ...), ...])` listing all declared fields.

### AC-4: Run a Solr query

- Given the same `products` collection with seeded data.
- When the operator POSTs `{target: "products", query_dsl: {q: "shirt", qf: "title^2 description^1", defType: "edismax"}, top_k: 5}` to `/run_query`.
- Then the response is 200 with `hits` of length ≤5, scores in descending order.

### AC-5: search_batch hits parallel `/select`, not serial

- Given a fresh Solr container with request logging on.
- When the adapter is called with `search_batch(target, queries=[q1, q2, q3, q4, q5], top_k=10)`.
- Then exactly 5 `/select` HTTP requests are made within a single overlapping time window (verified via response time + log timestamps; cassette assertion in the integration test).

### AC-6: UBI on Solr works through the existing UbiReader

- Given `local-solr` has `solr.UBIComponent` enabled and `ubi_queries` + `ubi_events` collections seeded from [`samples/ubi_index_mappings.json`](../../../../../samples/ubi_index_mappings.json) with synthetic clickstream.
- When the operator POSTs to `/api/v1/judgments/generate-from-ubi` with `{query_set_id, cluster_id: <local-solr-id>, target: "products", since: ..., until: ..., converter: "ctr_threshold"}`.
- Then a `judgment_list` is created with `source='click'` rows, no Solr-specific code path is exercised in the `UbiReader` (verify via call-graph assertion or absence of `engine_type` branching in the reader test), and the `judgment_lists.calibration` JSONB records the Solr engine version + UBI component version.

### AC-7: LTR rescore renders correctly + fails fast when model is missing

- Given `local-solr` has `xgboost_v1` uploaded to `/schema/model-store` and no other LTR models.
- When the operator creates a study with `search_space.params.rerank_model = {id: "xgboost_v1", top_k: 100}`.
- Then study create succeeds; the rendered Solr request includes `rq={!ltr model=xgboost_v1 reRankDocs=100}`.
- When the operator creates a study with `search_space.params.rerank_model = {id: "xgboost_v2", top_k: 100}`.
- Then study create rejects with 400 `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` and the message lists `["xgboost_v1"]` as available.

### AC-8: Frontend allowlist enforces source-of-truth discipline

- Given the `ENGINE_TYPE_VALUES` extension in `ui/src/lib/enums.ts` and a `// Values must match backend/app/adapters/protocol.py EngineType` discipline comment.
- When the form-select-discipline test runs ([`ui/src/__tests__/components/common/form-select-discipline.test.tsx`](../../../../../ui/src/__tests__/components/common/form-select-discipline.test.tsx)).
- Then the test passes (no inline `<SelectItem value="solr">` literals anywhere outside the `*_VALUES.map(...)` pattern).

### AC-9: Reversible migration

- Given the migration has been applied (`alembic upgrade head`) and no Solr cluster rows exist.
- When the operator runs `alembic downgrade -1`.
- Then the CHECK constraints revert to the pre-Solr allowlists; `alembic upgrade head` re-applies cleanly.
- Given the migration has been applied AND a Solr cluster row exists.
- When the operator runs `alembic downgrade -1`.
- Then the downgrade aborts with a clear error naming the row(s) that would violate the old constraint.

### AC-10: Full Karpathy loop against Solr (E2E)

- Given `local-solr` registered and a `products` collection with seeded data.
- When the operator runs the tutorial Step 0 Path C end-to-end via the UI (browser-driven Playwright test).
- Then a study runs against Solr, judgments are generated, a proposal is created, and a PR is opened against the config repo with Solr-engine-specific query parameters in the proposed config. (E2E spec: `ui/tests/e2e/solr-study-end-to-end.spec.ts`, real-backend, no `page.route()` mocking.)

### AC-11: `explain` round-trip

- Given `local-solr` `products` collection with seeded data and a query that matches at least one document.
- When the operator calls `SolrAdapter.explain(target, query, doc_id)` directly (unit/integration test boundary).
- Then the returned `ExplainTree` matches the unified shape: `doc_id`, `matched=true`, `value` matches the document's score, `description` contains a Solr-flavored explanation (e.g., starts with `"sum of:"`), and recursive `details` populate at least one level deep.

### AC-12: `get_document` via RealTime Get + uniqueKey discovery

- Given `local-solr` with a `products` collection whose `solrconfig.xml` declares `uniqueKey=sku` (not the default `id`), AND the cluster has been registered (`engine_config.unique_key_per_target["products"]="sku"` was persisted by the registration-time probe).
- When the operator hits `GET /api/v1/clusters/<local-solr-id>/documents/<sku-value>?target=products`.
- Then the SolrAdapter reads `unique_key_per_target["products"]="sku"` from `engine_config` (no schema probe needed — it ran at registration), issues `/products/get?id=<sku-value>`, and returns the document.
- And given a NEW collection `archive` created on Solr AFTER registration (not yet in `engine_config.unique_key_per_target`).
- When the operator hits `GET /api/v1/clusters/<local-solr-id>/documents/<id>?target=archive`.
- Then the SolrAdapter fetches `/archive/schema/uniquekey` and caches the result **in adapter memory only** (no DB write — service-layer-only write invariant). Subsequent calls during the adapter's lifetime skip the schema probe; persistence requires `POST /api/v1/clusters/<id>/reprobe`.

### AC-13: `list_documents` via cursorMark

- Given the same `products` collection with 100 documents.
- When the operator pages through `GET /api/v1/clusters/<id>/documents?target=products&limit=25` four times (consuming the `cursor` from each response).
- Then each response carries exactly 25 documents (page 4 may carry fewer if total isn't a multiple), `has_more=true` for pages 1–3, `has_more=false` for the last; no document repeats; the cursor pass-through into Solr's `cursorMark` is opaque to the client.

### AC-14: `/reprobe` updates `engine_config` atomically

- Given `local-solr` registered with `engine_config.ubi_component_present=false` (UBI plugin not yet installed at registration time).
- When the operator enables `solr.UBIComponent` in `solrconfig.xml`, restarts Solr, then POSTs `/api/v1/clusters/<id>/reprobe`.
- Then the response is 202 with the updated cluster row showing `engine_config.ubi_component_present=true`; the row's `updated_at` (or equivalent) advances; concurrent `/reprobe` calls coalesce on the row-level lock (no race).

### AC-15: `/healthz` extension reports Solr subsystem

- Given the local Compose stack is up with the `solr` service running.
- When the operator hits `GET /healthz`.
- Then the response includes `subsystems.solr = {status: "green", version: "10.0.0"}` alongside `subsystems.elasticsearch` and `subsystems.opensearch`; total response time stays under 500ms p99.
- Given the Compose `solr` service is stopped but the rest of the stack is up.
- When the operator hits `GET /healthz`.
- Then the response includes `subsystems.solr.status = "unreachable"` and the overall endpoint stays 200 (Solr-down doesn't fail healthz).

## 13) Non-functional requirements

- **Performance:** `search_batch` for 50 queries × top_k=10 against a 1,000-doc Solr collection completes in <2.5s p99 (local Compose Solr) — slightly looser than ES's <2s target because parallel `/select` lacks the request-coalescing of `_msearch`. Schema lookup completes in <500ms p99.
- **Reliability:** Adapter handles cluster restart cleanly — connection pool drops dead connections and retries once on `ConnectionError`. After a single retry, errors propagate via the named exception classes.
- **Operability:** Adapter logs every `search_batch` invocation with `cluster_id`, `target`, `query_count`, `top_k`, `duration_ms`, `request_id`, `engine="solr"` at INFO level. Failed requests at WARN. Per-`/select` failures inside a `strict_errors=False` batch logged at INFO (not WARN — they're expected hot-path failures).
- **Accessibility:** Solr engine badge has `aria-label="Apache Solr engine"`; tooltips keyboard-focusable.

## 14) Test strategy requirements

- **Unit tests** (`backend/tests/unit/adapters/`):
  - `test_solr_protocol.py` — `SolrAdapter` satisfies `isinstance(adapter, SearchAdapter)`.
  - `test_solr_render.py` — `render(template, params, query_text)` produces the expected Solr request-params dict for each canonical template; covers `edismax`, `dismax`, `lucene` parsers; covers every entry in the cross-engine parameter map (FR-4 bullets).
  - `test_solr_mm_syntax.py` — accepts unified `int | float | str`; validates against Solr's `mm` arithmetic syntax.
  - `test_solr_boost_fn_combine.py` — `combine="add"` renders to `bf`; `combine="multiply"` renders to `boost`.
  - `test_solr_ltr_rescore.py` — `rerank_model` renders to `rq={!ltr model=… reRankDocs=…}`; unknown model id surfaces as `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` (after probe).
  - `test_solr_capability_probe.py` — probe parses version, mode, UBI component presence, LTR module presence, LTR model list; persists to `engine_config`.
  - `test_solr_error_mapping.py` — 400 → `InvalidQueryDSLError`, 404 → `TargetNotFoundError`, 401/403 → `TargetsForbiddenError`, 5xx → `ClusterUnreachableError`, timeout → `QueryTimeoutError`.
  - `test_solr_auth_kinds.py` — `solr_basic` and `solr_apikey` construct correctly; mismatched pairings rejected by `ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE`.
- **Integration tests** (`backend/tests/integration/`) — **standalone Solr against the live Compose container:**
  - `test_solr_live_search.py` — real Compose Solr; full `search_batch` round-trip; `query_id` mapping preserved.
  - `test_solr_live_schema.py` — `get_schema` returns the seeded `products` schema correctly.
  - `test_solr_live_list_targets.py` — `list_targets` returns the `products` collection (and other seeded collections); `target_filter` glob works; system targets excluded.
  - `test_solr_live_explain.py` — `debugQuery=true` parses into the unified `ExplainTree`.
  - `test_solr_live_document_browser.py` — `get_document` + `list_documents` paginate correctly via `cursorMark`; uniqueKey probe runs and caches.
  - `test_solr_live_ltr_rescore.py` — pre-uploaded LTR model + `rerank_model` parameter; round-trip succeeds.
  - `test_solr_live_ubi_reader.py` — seeded `ubi_queries` + `ubi_events` collections; `UbiReader.scan()` returns events through the SolrAdapter without engine-specific branching.
  - `test_solr_live_reprobe.py` — `POST /api/v1/clusters/{id}/reprobe` updates `engine_config` atomically; concurrent calls coalesce.
  - `test_solr_live_healthz.py` — `/healthz` includes `subsystems.solr` with version + status; under-200ms timeout enforced.
  - `test_clusters_api_solr.py` — full create/list/detail/delete flow with `engine_type=solr`.
- **Integration tests — SolrCloud coverage (cassette / mocked HTTP, NOT live Compose):**
  - SolrCloud requires ZooKeeper, multi-node coordination, and a different list-targets API path (`CollectionsAdmin` vs `CoresAdmin`). Adding a live SolrCloud Compose profile is **out of scope for this feature** (would balloon CI runtime + Docker resource needs). Instead: `test_solr_cloud_list_targets_mocked.py` uses `pytest-recording` cassettes captured against a manually-set-up SolrCloud cluster to verify the `CollectionsAdmin` path; the capability-probe branch that selects cloud vs standalone is unit-tested with mocked HTTP responses (`test_solr_capability_probe.py`). The runbook documents the live-SolrCloud verification steps for the maintainer who records new cassettes. **This is a deliberate scope reduction with operator-visible coverage tradeoff** — surface in §19 decision log.
- **Contract tests** (`backend/tests/contract/`):
  - Extend [`backend/tests/contract/test_clusters_api_contract.py`](../../../../../backend/tests/contract/test_clusters_api_contract.py) with a Solr branch — request/response shapes match OpenAPI for `engine_type=solr`.
  - Extend [`backend/tests/contract/test_error_codes.py`](../../../../../backend/tests/contract/test_error_codes.py) with the new `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` (400) + the engine-agnostic codes routed via `SolrAdapter` — each produces the documented HTTP status + envelope shape.
- **Protocol-conformance unit test** (`backend/tests/unit/adapters/test_protocol.py` — existing): extend with a Solr branch asserting `isinstance(SolrAdapter(...), SearchAdapter)` is True and every method is `inspect.iscoroutinefunction` per the Protocol's async contract (sync exceptions: `render`, `list_query_parsers`).
- **E2E tests** (`ui/tests/e2e/`):
  - `solr-study-end-to-end.spec.ts` — full Karpathy loop against the live Compose Solr (real-backend, no `page.route()` mocking, per CLAUDE.md E2E Testing Rules). Register cluster → create study → generate LLM judgments → run trials → generate digest → open proposal → PR created.
  - Extend the existing cluster-registration E2E to assert the Solr engine badge renders.

## 16) Rollout and migration readiness

- **Feature flags:** None.
- **Migration/backfill:** One Alembic migration extending `clusters.engine_type` + `clusters.auth_kind` CHECKs. Backfill not required (no existing Solr rows possible; constraint extensions are pure relaxations).
- **Operational readiness gates:**
  - `docs/03_runbooks/solr-cluster-registration.md` merged.
  - `make up` brings up the new `solr` service on a fresh checkout without operator intervention.
  - `make seed-clusters` registers `local-solr` idempotently (extends the existing seed-clusters script).
  - The tutorial Step 0 Path C walkthrough works end-to-end on a clean machine.
- **Release gate:** All four test layers green; cross-model GPT-5.5 review clean; full Karpathy-loop E2E green against the live Compose Solr.

- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` updates (repo structure + ports + release matrix) merged.
- [ ] GPT-5.5 cross-model review converged within 3 cycles; all High-severity findings adjudicated.
