## 19) Open questions and decision log

### Open questions

None gating the plan. The two remaining open questions inherited from the idea both have recommended defaults that `/impl-plan-gen` can act on; they only flip status if integration testing surfaces contention.

1. **`HTTPX_POOL_LIMITS` settings-level tunable.** Status: **recommend deferred** (matches the locked decision in §3 Out of scope). Re-open only if `tests/integration/test_solr_live_search.py` shows contention under the planned parallelism (~50 queries × 5–10 concurrent trials on a single worker).
2. **LTR test fixture for the E2E.** Status: **recommend render-shape assertion** (cheap; deterministic; no model artifact in the repo). Per AC-7, the integration-level `test_solr_live_ltr_rescore.py` exercises an actual model upload + rescore round-trip against a checked-in fixture. The E2E spec asserts only that the rendered request payload contains `rq={!ltr ...}` — no need for a model in the E2E path.

### Decision log

- **2026-05-30** — **Template path locked at `samples/templates/<engine>/`** (matches `chore_template_library_expansion` + `mvp2-overview.md` §C1). Rationale: keep both adapters' templates under one root; the sibling chore already extends `samples/templates/`. Arch-doc drift in `mvp2-overview.md` Story A2 + A3 row (still says `templates/solr/`) flagged for this feature's Verification Ledger patch.
- **2026-05-30** — **`HTTPX_POOL_LIMITS` deferred.** Rely on the inline `httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=Timeout(10.0, connect=2.0))` default (`elastic.py:124`). Introduce a settings tunable only if integration tests show contention. Mirrors the ES adapter posture exactly.
- **2026-05-30** — **Single-phase delivery.** All 10 Workstream A stories ship in one PR; splitting any of {migration, Compose service, frontend allowlist, runbook} into a follow-up phase creates a half-shipped state (e.g., backend accepts Solr but UI rejects, or vice versa). Release-size estimate (~2–3 engineer-weeks solo) is small enough to ship atomically.
- **2026-05-30** — **LTR consume-only; training deferred.** Per `adapters.md` §"Reserved for later releases" — cross-engine LTR training is a Path A v2 capability. This feature renders the rescore stage and consumes a pre-existing `MultipleAdditiveTreesModel`; operators upload models via Solr's `/schema/model-store` out-of-band.
- **2026-05-30** — **All 10 Protocol methods in scope, not just the 7 the idea named.** `get_document` + `list_documents` (added by `feat_index_document_browser`) and `health_check` + `list_query_parsers` were undercounted in the idea; the live Protocol contract requires all 10 implementations or the document-browser UI breaks the moment a Solr cluster is selected.
- **2026-05-30** — **One new error code: `LTR_MODEL_NOT_FOUND`** (400). Every other Solr failure mode reuses an existing engine-agnostic code (`CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE`, `TARGET_NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_QUERY_DSL`, `QUERY_TIMEOUT`, `TARGETS_FORBIDDEN`). Adding more Solr-specific codes risks fragmenting the contract; keep the error envelope engine-agnostic where possible.
- **2026-05-30** — **`engine_config.mode` is `"cloud"` | `"standalone"`.** Source of truth in `backend/app/adapters/solr.py` (new constant `SOLR_MODE_VALUES`). Not user-facing as a typed value; surfaces only in the engine badge tooltip.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1)** — **Capability probe persistence moved from adapter constructor to service-layer probe-and-save step.** Original draft said "probe at construction writes to `engine_config`"; GPT-5.5 (F4) flagged the write-path invariant violation (adapter constructor has no DB session). Fix: `build_adapter()` is pure, the service layer calls `adapter.probe_capabilities()` returning a `ProbeResult` model, the service writes to `engine_config` in the same transaction as the cluster row INSERT/UPDATE. New endpoint `POST /api/v1/clusters/{id}/reprobe` added (AC-14) so operators can re-run after enabling UBI / loading LTR / etc. without re-creating the cluster.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1)** — **`UnsupportedParameter` mapped to existing `InvalidQueryDSLError`.** GPT-5.5 (F3) flagged the spec naming a new exception without defining its router translation. Decision: reuse `InvalidQueryDSLError` (which already maps to 400 `INVALID_QUERY_DSL`) with a Solr-flavored message. Keeps the router contract engine-agnostic; avoids introducing a new error code for what's morphologically a query-validation failure.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1)** — **Engine/auth allowlist relocation MANDATORY.** GPT-5.5 (F13) noted that keeping `SUPPORTED_AUTH_KINDS`/`ALLOWED_AUTH_PER_ENGINE` in `elastic.py` after adding a second adapter forces non-ES code to import ES internals. Decision: move to a new `backend/app/adapters/registry.py` module in this feature. `elastic.py` re-exports the names as deprecated aliases for one release to avoid churn on downstream imports. `SolrAdapter` imports from `registry.py`. Frontend `enums.ts` source-of-truth comments cite `registry.py`.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1)** — **`get_document`/`list_documents` use Solr RealTime Get + uniqueKey probe + cursorMark adapter-level encoding.** GPT-5.5 (F6) flagged that `q=id:<doc_id>` assumes uniqueKey is `id` and ignores Lucene escaping, while `cursorMark` is opaque (not per-hit, unlike Elastic's `search_after`). Fix: SolrAdapter probes `/schema/uniquekey` per target (cached in `engine_config.unique_key_per_target`), uses RealTime Get for `get_document`, and encodes Solr's `nextCursorMark` into the trailing has-more sentinel's `sort=[<nextCursorMark>]` so the unified Protocol contract survives unchanged.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1)** — **SolrCloud test coverage downgraded to cassette + mocked HTTP (NOT live Compose).** GPT-5.5 (F9) correctly flagged that the spec scopes both cloud + standalone but only tests standalone live. Adding a SolrCloud Compose profile requires ZooKeeper + multi-node coordination (CI runtime + resource cost). Decision: live integration tests cover standalone only; the `CollectionsAdmin` path is covered by `pytest-recording` cassettes captured against a maintainer-set-up SolrCloud instance + unit tests with mocked HTTP for the capability-probe branch. The runbook documents the cassette-recording procedure. **Operator-visible coverage tradeoff:** real-backend SolrCloud regression coverage relies on the cassette refresh cadence (re-record when Solr 10.x ships, when CollectionsAdmin shape changes, etc.).
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1)** — **Version-mismatch error reuses `CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE`** (not a new `ENGINE_VERSION_NOT_SUPPORTED` code). GPT-5.5 (F2) flagged the inconsistency. Decision: follow the ES adapter precedent (`infra_adapter_elastic/feature_spec.md` §11 edge flows handle "Engine version incompatibility" via `CLUSTER_UNREACHABLE`). Keeps the error code catalog smaller; the message field carries the human-readable version detail.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1)** — **`solr_apikey` `refresh_url` OUT OF SCOPE for MVP2.** GPT-5.5 (F5) noted refresh semantics were undefined. Decision: support `Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>` header only; credential rotation is operator-driven (update the mounted file → call `/reprobe`). Re-open if rotation friction becomes real.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1)** — **Compose `solr:10.0` pin + `/admin/info/system` healthcheck.** GPT-5.5 (F8) flagged image-tag contradiction (`solr:10` vs `solr:10.0.0`) and `/admin/ping` not working pre-core-creation. Decision: pin `solr:10.0`; healthcheck `curl -fsS http://localhost:8983/solr/admin/info/system` (verified to respond on fresh containers before any core exists).
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1)** — **`/healthz` `subsystems.solr` extension added as explicit FR-12a** (with AC-15). Originally mentioned only in the §2 current state audit; GPT-5.5 (F12) noted no FR/AC bound the contract change. Decision: dedicated FR with timeout enforcement (200ms per CLAUDE.md absolute rule #11), Solr-down does not fail the overall endpoint.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1, REJECTED)** — **GPT-5.5 F7 rejected (stale context).** GPT-5.5 flagged `feat_demo_ubi_study_comparison` PR #320 as possibly unmerged. Counter-evidence: `state.md:32` records "2026-05-30 — `feat_demo_ubi_study_comparison` Phase 1 (PR #320, squash-merged `853a5053`)" + `docs/00_overview/implemented_features/2026_05_30_feat_demo_ubi_study_comparison/` exists in tree. GPT-5.5 lacked the project context the reviewer prompt deliberately withheld.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 1, REJECTED)** — **GPT-5.5 F14 rejected (stale context).** GPT-5.5 flagged `feat_contextual_help` glossary dependency as possibly unshipped. Counter-evidence: `MVP2_DASHBOARD.md` "Done (2)" lists `feat_contextual_help_mvp2` merged 2026-05-15 (PR #124); `ui/src/lib/glossary.ts` is referenced multiple times in CLAUDE.md "Enumerated Value Contract Discipline" + the form-select-discipline lint guard. The glossary is shipped infrastructure.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 2)** — **`list_documents` uses `rows=<limit>` exactly, NOT overfetch.** GPT-5.5 cycle 2 F1 caught a real pagination data-loss bug: cycle-1's `rows=<limit+1>` overfetch + trailing-sentinel encoding would skip one document per page because Solr computes `nextCursorMark` based on ALL returned rows. Fix: request `rows=<limit>` exactly; surface `nextCursorMark` via a new optional `DocumentPage.next_cursor_token: str | None` Protocol field (additive — ES adapter returns `None`). Router cursor encoder consumes either `next_cursor_token` (Solr) or trailing hit's `sort` (ES). `has_more` derives from `next_cursor_token is not None` for Solr.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 2)** — **`DocumentPage.next_cursor_token` is a Protocol-shape change.** Additive: ES `DocumentPage` returns `next_cursor_token=None` (unchanged behavior); Solr returns the `nextCursorMark` string. Documented in §9 as a `protocol.py` change in this feature.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 2)** — **`UnsupportedParameter` removed from FR-4 entirely.** GPT-5.5 cycle 2 F2 caught the cycle-1 inconsistency (FR-1 said "use InvalidQueryDSLError" but FR-4 still named `UnsupportedParameter`). Fix: FR-4 now explicitly uses `InvalidQueryDSLError`; no new exception class. The string `UnsupportedParameter` does not appear in the spec.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 2)** — **uniqueKey enumeration moved to probe time** (not lazy from adapter). GPT-5.5 cycle 2 F3 caught the contradiction with the service-layer-only write invariant. Fix: `probe_capabilities()` enumerates current targets and populates `unique_key_per_target` for each. New targets created post-registration trigger an in-memory adapter-side fetch (lifetime-cached, NOT persisted); `POST /reprobe` persists newly-discovered targets. `engine_config.unique_key_per_target` is enumerated in §8.3 and §9 as a required field on Solr clusters.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 2)** — **Compose `security.json` generated by entrypoint script, not static-mounted.** GPT-5.5 cycle 2 F4 caught that a static `security.json` can't resolve Docker secret-file references (BasicAuth credentials need bcrypt hashes). Fix: `docker/solr/bootstrap-security.sh` runs at container startup, reads `*_FILE` secrets, calls `bin/solr auth enable-basic-auth` to generate the hashed credentials in `security.json`. `/admin/info/system` configured as an anonymous-allowlist endpoint so the unauthenticated healthcheck keeps working; everything else requires BasicAuth.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 2)** — **§8.4 `auth_kind` source of truth updated to cite `registry.py`** (not `elastic.py:70`). GPT-5.5 cycle 2 F5 caught the leftover citation that contradicted the FR-3 mandated relocation. Fix landed.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 2)** — **DoD checklist updated to AC-1 through AC-15** (was AC-1 through AC-10). GPT-5.5 cycle 2 F6 noted that the new cycle-1 ACs (11–15) for explain, document-browser get/list, reprobe, and `/healthz` Solr subsystem weren't part of the release gate.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 3 — CONVERGENCE; verdict "ready", 0 new High)** — **Solr `cursorMark` terminal-condition rule.** Cycle 3 C3-F1 caught that Solr returns `nextCursorMark` even on the last page (equal to the request's `cursorMark`). Fix in FR-9: set `next_cursor_token = nextCursorMark` only when `nextCursorMark != current_cursorMark`, else `None`. Without this the document browser would loop or report `has_more=true` forever.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 3)** — **FR-11 `security.json` mention realigned to bootstrap-script flow.** Cycle 3 C3-F2: leftover wording in FR-11 still said a pre-baked `security.json` was mounted. Fix: FR-11 now points at the FR-3 bootstrap-script flow; only the script is checked into the repo, no credential-bearing static file.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 3)** — **uniqueKey persistence wording fully normalized.** Cycle 3 C3-F3: FR-2 still said "populated lazily and cached" and AC-12 implied adapter-side `engine_config` writes. Fix: FR-2 ProbeResult now says "enumerated at probe time"; AC-12 explicitly distinguishes the registration-persisted case from the post-registration adapter-memory-only fetch (no DB write). Service-layer-only write invariant is now uncontradicted.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 3)** — **§8.3 example + §8.2 require `unique_key_per_target`.** Cycle 3 C3-F4: §9 marked the field required but §8.3 example omitted it. Fix: example now shows `"unique_key_per_target": {"products": "id"}`.
- **2026-05-30 (GPT-5.5 cycle 3)** — **`/healthz` Solr-subsystem presence rule clarified.** Cycle 3 C3-F5: FR-12a hinted both "configured-only" and "always-present-when-anything-tries-to-probe" semantics. Fix: present whenever `SOLR_HOST` is configured (config is the gate). Configured-but-unreachable → `unreachable` status (field still present). Only omit when `SOLR_HOST` is absent.
- **2026-05-30 — CONVERGENCE.** Cycle 3 verdict "ready" with 0 new High-severity findings; all cycle-3 Medium/Low findings patched in-cycle. Cross-model review loop closes per spec-gen stop rule.
